title: No Content-Length guard on remote fetches
description: Unbounded resp.bytes().await on actor/WebFinger fetches allows a malicious remote to send multi-gigabyte responses. Must check Content-Length against a cap (e.g. 1MB) before reading. Audit all outbound fetch call sites.
notes: CONFIRMED: 4 call sites buffer .bytes().await before post-hoc size check. Sites: actor_cache.rs:70, server.rs:863, feed.rs:43, media.rs:80. Fix requires streaming reader with byte cap (resp.bytes_stream() with accumulation limit). Content-Length pre-checks exist but are advisory only (server can omit/lie).
close_reason: Fixed: new src/http.rs read_body_limited() streams chunks via resp.chunk() with hard byte cap. Aborts immediately when limit exceeded — never allocates unbounded memory. Updated all 4 call sites: actor_cache.rs, server.rs (Follow handler), feed.rs, media.rs. All 13 tests passing.
